evidence: Each frame presents a single coherent interactive — the amber probe plus periwinkle candidates up top with their lilac score bars below — and the currently leading candidate is emphasized by a brighter bar and bold readout.
The elements are one probe, four candidate arrows, and four score bars belonging to a single scoring system, not five unrelated novel ideas competing at equal weight.
Candidates are labeled c1..c4 at their tips, the probe is labeled on its object, and each score bar carries a c1..c4 label directly beneath it with no legend or key.
The only prose is the short instruction 'Drag the probe to score each candidate...' which annotates the visible interactive rather than replacing it.
The instruction line, arrow labels, and mono score readouts are all rendered clearly enough to read at both the 1280 and 375 px widths.
In every state the arrows, labels, and score bars sit well inside the frame with generous margins on all sides.
Across states the probe label, candidate labels, and score readouts stay clear of one another; where the probe lies along a candidate (aligned frame) the labels remain radially offset and readable.
The amber probe (attention), periwinkle candidates (observed), and lilac score bars hold their colors consistently across all frames with no concept switching hue.
Every element — arrows, handles, bars, readouts, labels — carries propositional meaning; there is no decorative filler.

Each driven state matches its promise: initial probe up with c2/c3 leading, aligned c1 peaks at 1.0, orthogonal c3 peaks and c1 at 0.0, anti-aligned c1 reads -1.0, and length-cheat shows c1 stretched with score 1.3 flipping past c2's 1.0 while the probe stays on c2.
The score bars sit some distance below the arrow diagram, but shared c1..c4 labels bridge each bar to its candidate so the mapping is unambiguous.
These are separate driven states of the same interactive rather than a single built-up morph sequence; the probe repositioning reads consistently across them.
The layout has ample margins and a clear top-to-bottom reading order from arrows to score bars, though the lower portion of the frame is largely empty.
